question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getBucketName()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the importer to download tampered block stream files by poisoning the network-derived bucket name returned by `ImporterProperties.HederaNetwork.getBlockStreamBucketName()` when `bucketName` is blank, so that transactions in those blocks are silently replaced with attacker-controlled data, leading to corrupt transaction history being persisted to the mirror node database?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getBucketName()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of any integrity check on the resolved `bucketName` value to redirect block stream downloads to an attacker-controlled cloud storage bucket containing forged transaction records, given that `StringUtils.isNotBlank(bucketName)` accepts any non-blank string without validating bucket ownership or authenticity?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getBucketName()] Can an unprivileged external user who can influence environment variables or Spring Boot external configuration (e.g., via a misconfigured config server) set `hiero.mirror.importer.block.bucketName` to an attacker-controlled bucket, causing the importer to ingest tampered block files containing forged submitted transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getSourceType()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `sourceType = BlockSourceType.AUTO` default to force a fallback from `BLOCK_NODE` to `FILE` source by flooding the block node with errors, then serve tampered block files from a compromised cloud storage bucket, causing the importer to process attacker-crafted transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `autoDiscoveryEnabled = true` default combined with the `nodes` collection being initially empty to inject a malicious block node into the discovery database, causing the importer to stream tampered transaction blocks from an attacker-controlled endpoint?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user who registers a malicious block node in the `registeredNodeRepository` (via a public API path) have that node merged into the `nodes` collection through `autoDiscoveryEnabled` logic, then stream blocks with tampered submitted transactions to the importer?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getLedger()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `ledger` (LedgerProperties) is nullable (no `@NotNull`) to cause the importer to skip TSS/ledger-based block verification entirely, allowing tampered transaction blocks to be accepted without cryptographic proof validation?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getLedger()] Can an unprivileged external user who can influence the `hiero.mirror.importer.block.ledger` configuration cause the `historyProofVerificationKey` in `LedgerProperties` to be set to an attacker-controlled key, so that forged block proofs for tampered transactions pass verification?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getLedger()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `ledger` field being `@Valid` but not `@NotNull` to supply a `LedgerProperties` with an empty or zeroed `historyProofVerificationKey` byte array, bypassing TSS signature verification on block items containing submitted transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getStream()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `stream.maxBlockItems = 800_000` limit by crafting a block stream response that packs 800,000 items with attacker-controlled transaction data just under the limit, causing the importer to process a large batch of tampered transactions in a single block without triggering any size-based rejection?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getStream()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `stream.maxStreamResponseSize = 36MB` ceiling to deliver a maximally-sized block stream response filled with tampered transaction records, knowing the importer will accept and process the entire payload without per-transaction integrity checks at the stream layer?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `frequency = 100ms` polling interval to perform a timing attack where tampered block files are swapped into the cloud storage bucket between the importer's download and verification steps, causing a TOCTOU race that results in tampered transactions being persisted?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: isPersistBytes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit `persistBytes = false` (default) to cause the importer to discard raw block bytes after processing, preventing forensic detection of tampered transaction data that was already written to the database, effectively covering the attacker's tracks?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: isWriteFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit `writeFiles = false` (default) to ensure that tampered block stream files are never written to the filesystem, eliminating the audit trail that would otherwise allow detection of transaction tampering after the fact?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `enabled = false` default combined with a misconfigured deployment where block stream is enabled but ledger verification is not configured (null `ledger`), causing the importer to process blocks from an unverified source and accept tampered submitted transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a `BlockNodeProperties` node with `requiresTls = false` into the `nodes` collection via auto-discovery, causing the importer to stream transaction blocks over an unencrypted channel where a network-level attacker can perform a man-in-the-middle attack to tamper with submitted transactions in transit?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `getMergeKey()` collision logic (based on `host:port|requiresTls`) to register a discovered node that shares the same merge key as a legitimate config-file node, causing the attacker's node properties to silently override the legitimate node's configuration and redirect block streaming to a tampered transaction source?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the node priority ordering (`priority = 0` is highest) by registering a discovered block node with `priority = 0` that overrides all config-file nodes, ensuring the importer always streams from the attacker-controlled node first and processes tampered submitted transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getSourceType()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `AUTO` source type fallback mechanism in `CompositeBlockSource` — where after 3 consecutive errors the source switches from `BLOCK_NODE` to `FILE` — to deliberately cause block node errors and force the importer to fall back to a tampered cloud storage bucket containing forged transaction blocks?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getStream()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit `stream.maxSubscribeAttempts = 3` by causing exactly 3 subscribe failures on all legitimate block nodes, forcing the importer to mark all nodes inactive and fall back to the FILE source backed by an attacker-controlled bucket containing tampered transaction data?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getStream()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `stream.readmitDelay = 1 minute` window — during which all legitimate block nodes are inactive — to serve tampered block files from the FILE source, causing the importer to process attacker-crafted transactions for up to 60 seconds before any legitimate node is readmitted?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getStream()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `stream.responseTimeout = 400ms` threshold by causing legitimate block nodes to respond just above 400ms (e.g., via network congestion or DDoS), triggering timeouts that mark nodes inactive and force fallback to a tampered FILE source containing forged submitted transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getBucketName()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `getBucketName()` has no validation of the returned bucket name format or ownership to supply a bucket name pointing to a public cloud storage bucket they control, causing the importer to download and process tampered block files with forged transaction records?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getLedger()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `nodeContributions` list in `LedgerProperties` being `@NotEmpty` only at the `@Builder.Default` level (not enforced when set via Spring config binding) to supply an empty `nodeContributions` list, causing TSS weight calculations to fail or be bypassed, allowing tampered transaction blocks to pass ledger verification?
